hook.text: Every building is a geometry lesson. Arches, domes, and columns all follow mathematical rules.

buildup.text: Roman arches distribute weight evenly using semicircles. Gothic arches use pointed geometry.

discovery.text: A catenary arch—the shape of a hanging chain—is the strongest possible arch form.

twist.text: Modern architects use parametric geometry, letting computers generate shapes humans never imagined.

climax.text: From the Parthenon to the Sydney Opera House, geometry defines what buildings can become.

punchline.text: Geometry decides what stands up and what falls down.

quiz.question: What makes a catenary arch special?
options: It is the strongest possible arch shape
